page: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is equipped with 154 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU has a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography enabled, though it lacks AVX, AMX, and hyper-threading capabilities.

Benchmark data indicates average performance in several key areas, including stress-ng single-core (1822.59) and multi-core (7252.87) CPU tests, as well as single-threaded gzip compression (24.2 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (8.82 GB/sec). Large-block memory bandwidth is also average, measuring 51.33 GB/sec for read and 30.11 GB/sec for write operations. However, the server exhibits weak performance in Geekbench single-core (1091.0) and multi-core (3710.0) tests, software compilation, and ray tracing. Cryptographic performance is poor, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 651.08, and small-block cached memory bandwidth is weak at 282.27 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture provides a balanced resource density of vCPUs to memory at a lower hardware class tier, making it a viable alternative to x86 instances for compatible software stacks. Given its average performance in database operations (2662.23 in Passmark) and text processing, but weak results in LLM inference and web serving throughput, this server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight database hosting, text processing pipelines, and basic utility services that do not require high-performance vector or cryptographic calculations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the DPDSv5 family, utilizing a 4-core Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of RAM and 154 GB of local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show average performance in stress-ng CPU tests, large-block memory bandwidth, and database operations, but reveal weaknesses in cryptographic execution, small-block memory bandwidth, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Lacking AVX and hyper-threading, this server offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for ARM-compatible software. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, text processing, and lightweight database workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual server featuring 4 Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage, offering balanced general-purpose performance for ARM-compatible workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4pds_v5 is a 4-core ARM64 virtual machine with 16 GB RAM and 154 GB local storage, optimized for general-purpose ARM workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-powered virtual server with dedicated cores and local storage, delivering balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB RAM and 154 GB of local SSD storage
Delivers average performance in database operations and text processing
Shows performance limitations in cryptographic and vector-heavy tasks
Suitable for general-purpose applications and lightweight database hosting
